video:video.video:title: ICT building at Tehran's Sharif University of Technology reduced to rubble after more US-Israeli strikes
video:video.video:description: An airstrike hit Monday an information and communication technology building at Tehran's Sharif University of Technology, a professor there said.

video:video.video:title: Road to Tehran offers glimpses of destruction and daily life in wartime Iran
video:video.video:description: Associated Press reporters crossed into Iran from Turkey on Saturday after being granted permission by the Iranian government to send an additional team into the country.

video:video.video:title: Blind Massachusetts man is making it possible for those who can't see to build hundreds of Lego sets
video:video.video:description: Matthew Shifrin is making it possible for those who can't see to build hundreds of Lego sets using free accessibility instructions created by sighted and blind volunteers in his nonprofit group.

video:video.video:title: Israeli authorities recover bodies from building hit by Iranian missile
video:video.video:description: Israeli rescuers on Monday removed bodies of people from the rubble of a building hit by an Iranian missile in Haifa on Sunday.

video:video.video:title: AP Top Stories April 6
video:video.video:description: Here’s the latest for Monday April 6th: U.S. and Iran received draft proposal for ceasefire; More strikes in Iran and Israel; Wounded U.S. aviator rescued; Artemis II to swing around Moon on Monday.

video:video.video:title: From shore to plate: How Irish seaweed is making waves on restaurant menus
video:video.video:description: Irish seaweed is in demand. Traditionally used in fertilisers, chefs and researchers on Ireland's west coast are advocating for wider uses: on menus, in gardens and cosmetics.

video:video.video:title: Netanyahu offers condolences for four people killed in Iranian attack on Haifa
video:video.video:description: Israeli Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu on Monday expressed his condolences over victims killed by an Iranian missile attack in Haifa.

video:video.video:title: Unique Easter tradition lives on in Hungarian village as women doused in buckets of water
video:video.video:description: The small village of Hollókő in northeastern Hungary still observers an ancient Easter tradition to this day, when local men douse women with buckets of water during the holiday.

video:video.video:title: Savannah Guthrie returns to 'Today' anchor desk for first time since mother's disappearance
video:video.video:description: Savannah Guthrie has returned to NBC’s “Today” show anchor desk for the first time since her mother's disappearance more than two months ago.

video:video.video:title: Trump marvels at drop in price of eggs at White House Easter Egg roll
video:video.video:description: President Donald Trump and first lady Melania Trump welcomed thousands to the annual White House Easter Egg roll Monday.

video:video.video:title: Trump praises 'historic' US airman rescue, warns Iran could be 'taken out in one night'
video:video.video:description: Trump praises 'historic' US airman rescue, warns Iran could be 'taken out in one night'

video:video.video:title: Artemis crew can use the toilet as usual, NASA says, after reporting smell from bathroom
video:video.video:description: The toilet on Artemis II can be used as normal, entry flight director Rick Henfling said. It malfunctioned following Wednesday's liftoff and has been hit-or-miss ever since.

video:video.video:title: Researchers in Mexico develop easy-to-use robotic arm
video:video.video:description: Researchers in Mexico have developed a robotic arm they say reduces the complexity that often leads patients to abandon prosthetic devices.

video:video.video:title: Trump insists Iranians want the US to 'keep bombing'
video:video.video:description: President Donald Trump said Monday the Iranian people want the U.S. to "keep bombing," insisting citizens are “willing to suffer" in order to secure their freedom.

video:video.video:title: AP top stories April 6
video:video.video:description: Here's the latest for Monday, April 6th: Trump praises rescue of U.S. airmen in Iran; Trump and first lady take part in White House egg roll; former governor of Russia's Kursk region sentenced to prison; astronauts set record as the father humans have ever traveled from earth.

video:video.video:title: Pressure mounts on Kanye West to be pulled from headline role at summer music festival in London
video:video.video:description: Pressure was mounting Sunday on the American rapper Kanye West to be pulled from his headline role at a London music festival this summer.

video:video.video:title: Like her 'Something Bad' character, Camila Morrone listens to her gut
video:video.video:description: Camila Morrone trusts her instincts when making big decisions. She stars in the Netflix horror series "Something Very Bad is Going to Happen."

video:video.video:title: Trump brushes off war crimes concerns as he widens threat to Iran's power plants and bridges
video:video.video:description: U.S. President Donald Trump said Monday he’s “not at all” concerned about committing possible war crimes as he threatened to destroy Iran’s bridges and power plants if Tehran does not meet his Tuesday 8 p.m.

video:video.video:title: Dani Bowman talks life beyond ‘Love on the Spectrum’
video:video.video:description: "Can you believe it?" Here's what Dani Bowman says happened after appearing on the Netflix reality series "Love on the Spectrum."

video:video.video:title: Artemis II crew share emotional moment over loved one lost after breaking record
video:video.video:description: Moments after breaking Apollo 13’s record, the astronauts asked permission to name two fresh lunar craters already observed.

video:video.video:title: Artemis II breaks Apollo 13’s distance record as humans travel farther from Earth than ever before
video:video.video:description: The previous title was held by the Apollo 13 crew, who logged a maximum distance of 248,655 miles from Earth.

video:video.video:title: Video brings new scrutiny to ICE shooting in Minneapolis after charges against 2 men are dropped
video:video.video:description: Federal authorities initially accused two men of beating an ICE officer with a broom handle and a snow shovel before opening an investigation into the officers giving untruthful testimony under oath.

video:video.video:title: Iran to take 'serious legal steps' to protect its cultural sites
video:video.video:description: Iranian Minister of Cultural Heritage, Tourism and Handicrafts Sayed Reza Salihi-Amiri said on Sunday that Iran would take "serious legal steps" to protect its cultural sites, after Israeli and U.S. bombardments have damaged over 100 sites across the country.

video:video.video:title: Storms and heavy rainfall disrupt transportation in Afghanistan.
video:video.video:description: Storms and heavy rainfall began across Afghanistan about 12 days ago, affecting most of the country’s 34 provinces.

video:video.video:title: Turkey is striving to bring about an end to the 'unlawful, meaningless' war in Iran, Erdogan says
video:video.video:description: Turkey’s President Recep Tayyip Erdogan said his country has intensified a push to end "the unlawful, meaningless" war in Iran.

video:video.video:title: Iran official calls for youths to gather at power plants ahead of threatened Trump strikes
video:video.video:description: An Iranian official early Tuesday issued a video message calling on youths of the Islamic Republic to form "human chains" around power plants in the country ahead of threatened strikes by U.S.

video:video.video:title: Three, including one baby, die after tree falls during Easter egg hunt in German town
video:video.video:description: Three people, including a 10-month-old girl, were killed Sunday when high winds toppled a tree in northern Germany during an Easter egg hunt, police said.

video:video.video:title: Artemis II astronauts have 'extraordinary human experience' with daring moon flyby
video:video.video:description: After traveling deeper into space than any other humans, the Artemis II astronauts pointed their moonship toward home Monday night, wrapping up a lunar cruise that revealed views of the far side never beheld by eyes until now.

video:video.video:title: Artemis II astronauts speak with President Trump after moon flyby
video:video.video:description: The four astronauts on board the Artemis II spoke with President Donald Trump on after their record-breaking trip around the moon.

video:video.video:title: Dan Levy is making his own dreams come true
video:video.video:description: The "Schitt's Creek" creator jokes he writes the scripts for the genres he hopes to act in while promoting his new action/comedy.

video:video.video:title: Bangladesh conducts emergency measles vaccinations as outbreak kills more than 100 children
video:video.video:description: Bangladesh is conducting emergency measles-rubella vaccinations while trying to contain an ongoing outbreak that has killed more than 100 children in less than a month.

video:video.video:title: Another teen runs for governor in Vermont, this time making it to the general election ballot
video:video.video:description: Looking back, gubernatorial candidate Dean Roy says his political ambitions started in the eighth grade. And by that he means, last year.

video:video.video:title: Former Australian soldier charged with committing five war crime murders in Afghanistan
video:video.video:description: Australia’s most decorated living veteran, Ben Roberts-Smith, faces war crime charges on allegations that he killed five unarmed Afghans while serving in Afghanistan from 2009 and 2012, police and media reported on Tuesday.

video:video.video:title: Artemis II breaks Apollo 13’s distance record with daring moon flyby that included a solar eclipse
video:video.video:description: After traveling deeper into space than any other humans, the Artemis II astronauts pointed their moon ship toward home Monday night, wrapping up a lunar cruise that revealed views of the far side never beheld by eyes until now.

video:video.video:title: Why Cameron Diaz doesn't Google herself
video:video.video:description: Cameron Diaz stars in new dark comedy "Outcome" - a film about fame and cancel culture. In a recent interview with The Associated Press, she reflects on her own experience of living life in the spotlight.

video:video.video:title: Artemis astronauts woke up the voice of the late Apollo 13 commander Jim Lovell
video:video.video:description: On day 6 of their mission to the moon, the Artemis II astronauts woke up to the voice of Apollo 13 commander Jim Lovell, who recorded the message just two months before his death last August.

video:video.video:title: Artemis II breaks distance record as humans travel farther from Earth than ever before
video:video.video:description: Artemis II kicks off trip around the moon after surpassing Apollo 13’s distance record. Artemis II astronauts also got a special message from Apollo 13 commander Jim Lovell, who recorded the message just two months before his death last August.

video:video.video:title: Artemis II kicks off its trip around the moon
video:video.video:description: The Artemis II astronauts have started their record-breaking trip around the moon that already is providing unprecedented views of the far side.

video:video.video:title: Trump says it would take 4 hours to destroy Iran’s bridges and power plants
video:video.video:description: President Donald Trump described the consequences that Iran would face if it didn’t reach a deal with the U.S. by Trump’s 8 p.m. Eastern Tuesday deadline.

video:video.video:title: Trump says it would take 4 hours to destroy Iran’s bridges and power plants
video:video.video:description: The president described the consequences that Iran would face if it didn’t reach a deal with the U.S. by Trump’s 8 p.m. Tuesday deadline.

video:video.video:title: Trump says he's 'not at all' concerned about war crimes as he threatens Iran's infrastructure
video:video.video:description: President Donald Trump said Monday he’s “not at all” concerned about committing possible war crimes as he continued to threaten the destruction of Iran’s bridges and power plants if they don’t meet his Tuesday 8 p.m.

video:video.video:title: Anna Cathcart on Lana Condor's ‘XO, Kitty’ appearance
video:video.video:description: Lana Condor revives her "To All the Boys" character for Season 3 of "XO, Kitty." Here's what Anna Cathcart had to say about it.

video:video.video:title: Are we alone? Why the question of alien life keeps returning
video:video.video:description: Are we alone? From Trump’s push to release UFO files to new NASA missions, interest in alien life is back in the spotlight. Here’s why.

video:video.video:title: Funeral held for man killed in Israeli drone attack on Gaza City
video:video.video:description: An Israeli drone killed a Palestinian man Monday in the Gaza Strip, hospital authorities said. The strike hit a bike in the Sheikh Radwan neighborhood in Gaza City and also wounded a child, according to the Shifa hospital which received the casualties.

video:video.video:title: Retirees find gig work to help pay for bills
video:video.video:description: After concluding decades-long careers at hospitals, universities and corporations, retirees are returning to the workforce due to insufficient retirement savings, rising living costs and a desire to stay active.

video:video.video:title: Pope Leo XIV remembers his predecessor in Easter Monday blessing
video:video.video:description: Pope Leo XIV remembered his predecessor Pope Francis’s "profound witness of faith and love" during the traditional Easter Monday blessing.

video:video.video:title: University buildings in Tehran destroyed in US-Israeli strike as Trump ultimatum nears
video:video.video:description: Israel and the United States carried out a wave of attacks on Iran on Monday, killing more than 25 people.

video:video.video:title: Israel Katz says Israel has attacked Iran's South Pars gas field
video:video.video:description: Katz says Israel has attacked Iran's South Pars gas field

video:video.video:title: Record low snowpack in Colorado could spell trouble for millions in the U.S. West
video:video.video:description: Hydrologists working high in the Rocky Mountains have measured what they say was Colorado’s worst snowpack on record—an alarming fact for a state that supplies drinking water to millions across the drought-stricken American West.

video:video.video:title: Pope Leo XIV remembers Pope Francis in Easter Monday blessing
video:video.video:description: Pope Leo XIV remembered his predecessor Pope Francis’s "profound witness of faith and love" during the traditional Easter Monday blessing.

video:video.video:title: People in Tehran face coming to terms with daily bombardment by US and Israel
video:video.video:description: As the war in Iran enters its sixth week, residents of Tehran struggle to deal with the strikes in the city that has been heavily targeted.

video:video.video:title: Former Australian soldier charged with committing five war crime murders in Afghanistan
video:video.video:description: Australia's most decorated living veteran, Ben Roberts-Smith, faces war crime charges on allegations that he killed five unarmed Afghans while serving in Afghanistan from 2009 and 2012, police and media reported Tuesday.

video:video.video:title: Dan Levy is an action star (and he loves it)
video:video.video:description: Dan Levy wanted to be an action star, so he wrote new TV series 'Big Mistakes'

video:video.video:title: Video shows gunfight between attackers and police outside Israeli Consulate in Istanbul
video:video.video:description: Video shows gunfight between attackers and police outside Israeli Consulate in Istanbul

video:video.video:title: Lagos hosts Fanti Carnival celebrating Afro-Brazilian heritage
video:video.video:description: Colourful costumes and street processions filled the heart of Lagos Island as the Fanti Carnival returned, showcasing a unique cultural bridge between West Africa and Brazil.

video:video.video:title: Why Cameron Diaz doesn't Google herself
video:video.video:description: "Outcome" star Cameron Diaz reflects on life in the spotlight and why she doesn't Google herself.

video:video.video:title: One killed and more than a dozen injured in train crash at level crossing in Northern France
video:video.video:description: A high-speed train driver was killed on Tuesday in northern France when his locomotive collided into a truck at a level crossing in Northern France.

video:video.video:title: Rapper Offset shot and stable after incident at Seminole Hard Rock in Florida
video:video.video:description: The rapper Offset was shot in Florida, according to a spokesperson. The Seminole Police Department said a person sustained injuries that were not life-threatening Monday evening at a valet area at the Seminole Hard Rock in Hollywood, Florida.

video:video.video:title: Massive warehouse fire in southern California; one person arrested
video:video.video:description: An arson suspect was arrested after a massive fire erupted at a warehouse in Ontario; more than 100 firefighters were on the scene battling the blaze

video:video.video:title: Nutella jar seen spinning through Artemis II
video:video.video:description: Minutes before the Artemis II mission shattered the distance record set by Apollo 13 in 1970 a jar of Nutella hazelnut spread was seen spinning through the spacecraft.

video:video.video:title: As Trump threatens Iran's infrastructure, a Tehran couple wonders how to prepare
video:video.video:description: A couple living in Tehran prepared for the worst following U.S. President Donald Trump's threats to unleash "all Hell" in a bombing campaign targeting energy and civilian infrastructure across Iran.

video:video.video:title: UK government blocks Ye from entering Britain
video:video.video:description: The rapper formerly known as Kanye West has been barred from entering the U.K., where he was scheduled to perform at the Wireless Festival in July.

video:video.video:title: Ranbir Kapoor Leads Ambitious Epic 'Ramayana' I AP Interview
video:video.video:description: Ranbir Kapoor says that while he doesn't love the promotion side of his job, he's happy to do it for "Ramayana."

video:video.video:title: Vance in Budapest says he's confident Trump will get a response from Iran by deadline
video:video.video:description: Vance in Budapest says he's confident Trump will get a response from Iran by deadline

video:video.video:title: AP reporter tries becoming a tailor
video:video.video:description: AP's retail reporter Anne D'Innocenzio learned to hem a pair of jeans by Nordstrom tailor Marco Lema.

video:video.video:title: Celebrations in Ann Arbor after Michigan wins NCAA men's basketball national title
video:video.video:description: Students celebrated in the streets of Ann Arbor, Michigan on Monday night, after the University of Michigan Wolverines men’s basketball team won the NCAA championship.

video:video.video:title: 'The Testaments' stars hope their show inspires like 'Handmaid's'
video:video.video:description: Hulu's "The Testaments" - starring Lucy Halliday and Chase Infiniti - is a sequel to hit dystopian drama "The Handmaid's Tale."

video:video.video:title: Russia and China veto UN resolution aimed at reopening the Strait of Hormuz as US deadline for Iran deal looms
video:video.video:description: Russia and China on Tuesday vetoed a U.N. Security Council resolution aimed at reopening the Strait of Hormuz that had been repeatedly watered down in hopes those two countries would abstain.

video:video.video:title: Two killed in drone attack on civilian house in Irbil, authorities say
video:video.video:description: Two people were killed after an explosive-laden drone launched from Iran struck a civilian home in Irbil province, Iraq early Tuesday, authorities said.

video:video.video:title: 'Ketamine Queen' who supplied drugs to Matthew Perry set for sentencing
video:video.video:description: A woman who sold actor Matthew Perry the drug ketamine that killed him is set to be sentenced Wednesday.

video:video.video:title: Residents of Lebanese Christian village in shock following deadly Israeli airstrike
video:video.video:description: The hilly Christian town of Ain Saadeh east of Beirut was in shock Monday after an Israeli airstrike crashed into an apartment building there, killing an anti-Hezbollah politician, his wife and another woman.

video:video.video:title: Tension remain high at Israel-Lebanon border as Hezbollah attacks continue to set off sirens
video:video.video:description: Tensions remained high on the Israeli-Lebanese border as rocket and drone attacks by the Iran-backed militant group Hezbollah continued to set off sirens throughout the day on Tuesday.

video:video.video:title: Israelis on alert amid sound of sirens as Trump ultimatum for Iran approaches
video:video.video:description: Israelis on alert amid sound of sirens as Trump ultimatum for Iran approaches

video:video.video:title: US condemns Russia and China for veto on UN resolution aimed at reopening Strait of Hormuz
video:video.video:description: U.S. ambassador to the United Nations Mike Waltz assailed the countries that objected to a U.N. Security Council resolution aimed at reopening the Strait of Hormuz.

video:video.video:title: Why Jonah Hill chose Keanu Reeves to be canceled in 'Outcome'
video:video.video:description: Director Jonah Hill reveals why he cast Keanu Reeves in his new dark comedy, "Outcome," as an actor struggling to keep his head above water bwhen being blackmailed over a compromising video.

video:video.video:title: 'Charlie's Angels' cast celebrates 50th anniversary
video:video.video:description: "Charlie's Angels" Kate Jackson, Jaclyn Smith and Cheryl Ladd reunite to celebrate the show’s 50th anniversary at PaleyFest LA. (April 7)

video:video.video:title: Wisconsin QB Colton Joseph says he wants to show what he can do
video:video.video:description: Wisconsin quarterback and Old Dominion transfer Colton Joseph said the promise of offensive coordinator Jeff Grimes' system mattered more to him than the Badgers' recent struggles as he was deciding on a new school

video:video.video:title: Miniature art transforms an alley into a surrealist wonderland at "Mississippi's Tiniest Museum"
video:video.video:description: The Hattiesburg Pocket Museum, which started as a way to spread joy during the covid-19 pandemic, has since drawn more than 300,000 visitors eager to explore the alley's often colorful, sometimes hidden displays.

video:video.video:title: Acting AG Blanche insists it's Trump's 'right' and 'duty' to investigate adversaries
video:video.video:description: In his first news conference as acting U.S. attorney general, Todd Blanche dismissed accusations the Justice Department has been weaponized, insisting President Donald Trump has a "right" and "duty" to seek investigations against those he believes have wronged him.

video:video.video:title: Wall Street swings sharply as uncertainty increases about what will happen in the war with Iran
video:video.video:description: The S&P 500 fell as much as 1.2% Tuesday, hours ahead of a deadline by President Donald Trump to destroy Iranian power plants and bridges.

video:video.video:title: Pope says Trump's threat to destroy Iranian civilization is 'truly unacceptable'
video:video.video:description: Pope Leo XIV said Tuesday that U.S. President Donald Trump’s threat to destroy Iranian civilization was “truly unacceptable” and said any attacks on civilian infrastructure violate international law.

video:video.video:title: Palestinians queue for hours in pouring rain to get bread, amid food shortage in war torn Gaza
video:video.video:description: Palestinians in the Gaza Strip are struggling to meet their daily bread needs amid shortages of supplies entering the enclave and a lack of money.

video:video.video:title: Trump's threats to destroy Iran power plants raise war crime questions
video:video.video:description: President Donald Trump threatened to blow up every bridge and power plant in Iran, action that would be so far-reaching that some experts in military law said it could constitute a war crime.

video:video.video:title: Life in Tehran continues ahead of Trump deadline to attack power plants
video:video.video:description: Life appeared normal in Tehran hours before a deadline set by U.S President Donald Trump to attack the country's infrastructure if Iran does not agree to a deal.

video:video.video:title: Brazil's Indigenous leaders rally as land disputes and mining pressures grow
video:video.video:description: Thousands of Indigenous people marched in Brazil’s capital on Tuesday to protest what they say are violations of their land rights by large corporations advancing farming, logging and mining projects.

video:video.video:title: Iranians form human chains around power plants to protect them from US and Israeli attacks
video:video.video:description: People gathered around power plants in Iran on Tuesday as they answered the government's call to form human chains to protect the country's infrastructure.

video:video.video:title: Video brings new scrutiny to Minneapolis ICE shooting after charges collapse
video:video.video:description: The city of Minneapolis released a video showing a chase and scuffle that ended in a nonfatal shooting in January and the suspensions of two federal officers involved in the Trump administration’s immigration crackdown in Minnesota.

video:video.video:title: Former Migos rapper Offset shot outside a Florida casino
video:video.video:description: Offset, a former member of the influential hip-hop trio Migos, was shot outside a Florida casino and is in stable condition, a spokesperson said Tuesday.

video:video.video:title: AP top stories April 7
video:video.video:description: Here's the latest for Tuesday, April 7th: Trump warns ‘whole civilization will die tonight’ if Iran deal isn't reached; Vance visits Hungary in support of Orbán; gunmen attack building housing Israeli Consulate in Istanbul; Brazil's Indigenous leaders take to the streets.

video:video.video:title: Displaced Lebanese find shelter on covered football pitch in Sidon
video:video.video:description: A covered football stadium in the town of Haret Saida has been turned into an emergency shelter as Lebanon struggles with a growing displacement crisis.

video:video.video:title: Inside a massive online scam compound on Thailand-Cambodia border
video:video.video:description: Thailand’s military on Tuesday invited journalists back to the huge scam complex that it had seized in December during its border conflict with Cambodia.

video:video.video:title: Brenda Song's Disney Channel 'Mount Rushmore'
video:video.video:description: Brenda Song shares her Disney Channel “Mount Rushmore” while promoting season 2 of her latest project, “Running Point.”

video:video.video:title: Artemis II astronauts make long-distance call to the space station as they head home from the moon
video:video.video:description: Still aglow from their triumphant lunar flyby, the Artemis II astronauts put in a call to their friends aboard the International Space Station as they headed home from the moon.

video:video.video:title: Michigan fans gather outside basketball arena in Ann Arbor to welcome the 2026 national champs home
video:video.video:description: Michigan fans gathered outside Crisler Center to welcome the freshly crowned 2026 men’s basketball national champions back to Ann Arbor (AP video: Mike Householder)

video:video.video:title: Donald Trump Jr. criticizes the European Union during a trip to Bosnia
video:video.video:description: Donald Trump Jr. lashed out at the European Union on Tuesday, saying its liberal policies were discouraging investment and predicted a “major fracture” between the bloc's eastern and western member states.

video:video.video:title: Cuban women protest against US sanctions and embargo
video:video.video:description: Hundreds of Cuban women gathered in a Havana park on Tuesday to protest against US policies toward the Caribbean island, the historic economic embargo, and especially the energy blockade imposed by US President Donald Trump.

video:video.video:title: Rising fuel costs present 'huge issue' to German freight company
video:video.video:description: Freight companies are suffering as fuel prices in Germany rose to record highs, with the nationwide average price for diesel Tuesday exceeding 2.50 euros ($2.89).

video:video.video:title: Bryan Cranston shares memories daughter, Taylor Dearden, growing up on “Malcolm in the Middle” set.
video:video.video:description: At the New York premiere of the “Malcolm In The Middle” revival, Bryan Cranston shared memories of his daughter, Taylor Dearden of “The Pitt”, hanging out with the child cast of “Malcolm In The Middle.”

video:video.video:title: Iran's UN envoy says Tehran will not 'stand idle' if Trump follows through on threats
video:video.video:description: Iran's representative to the U.N. said Tehran would “take immediate and proportionate” action if President Donald Trump follows through on his threats to attack the country’s “whole civilization” if Iran does not make a deal and reopen the Strait of Hormuz

video:video.video:title: Iranian hospital reports stable services, stockpiled supplies despite war concerns
video:video.video:description: As the US and Israel enter their sixth week of airstrikes on Iran, many hospitals in Tehran continue to operate and serve patients.

video:video.video:title: Iran says it has accepted a two-week ceasefire in the war
video:video.video:description: U.S. President Donald Trump pulled back on his threats to launch devastating strikes on Iran late Tuesday, swerving to deescalate the war less than two hours before the deadline he set for Tehran to capitulate or else a “whole civilization will die.”

video:video.video:title: Trump-endorsed Republican Clay Fuller wins Marjorie Taylor Greene’s former House seat in Georgia
video:video.video:description: Republican Clay Fuller has won Marjorie Taylor Greene’s former U.S. House seat in Georgia. Fuller’s special election win Tuesday bolsters a slim GOP majority.

video:video.video:title: Asia market jumps up on news of US and Iran agreeing to 2-week ceasefire
video:video.video:description: Oil prices plunged and U.S. stock futures jumped after U.S. President Donald Trump held off on his threat of devastating attacks on Iran.

video:video.video:title: Route 66, a quintessential American road trip heavy on kitsch and history, turns 100
video:video.video:description: Route 66 marks its 100th anniversary this year. Despite losing its status decades ago as one of the nation’s main arteries, people around the world still flock to it to take the quintessential American road trip (AP Video: Ty ONeil, Julio Cortez, Susan Montoya Bryan, Jae Hong)

video:video.video:title: Gaza woman scrapes living making and selling dolls to bring some joy to kids in war-ravaged territory
video:video.video:description: Palestinians in the Gaza Strip are using arts and crafts to express their trauma from the war and earn an income, as hopes of returning to normal life remain limited with the territory’s humanitarian needs being overshadowed by the Iran war.

video:video.video:title: Republican Clay Fuller wins election to U.S. House in Georgia’s 14th District
video:video.video:description: Republican Clay Fuller on Tuesday won Marjorie Taylor Greene’s former U.S. House seat in Georgia, turning back a Democratic challenge with the help of President Donald Trump’s endorsement despite uneasiness over the war in Iran.

video:video.video:title: 'Euphoria' lead cast reunites for season three premiere
video:video.video:description: Zendaya, Jacob Elordi, Sydney Sweeney, Maude Apatow, Hunter Schafer and Alexa Demie reunited on the red carpet for the season three premiere of "Euphoria." (Apr. 7)

video:video.video:title: US and Iran agree to 2-week ceasefire as Trump pulls back on threats
video:video.video:description: Iran, the United States and Israel reached a tentative, two-week ceasefire Wednesday in the war that tore across the Middle East and disrupted the global energy market, with U.S.

video:video.video:title: Zendaya attends 'Euphoria' premiere in LA
video:video.video:description: Zendaya strutted down the carpet on Hollywood Boulevard to celebrate "Euphoria's" season three premiere in Los Angeles, Tuesday. (April 7)

video:video.video:title: US, Iran say they have agreed to a two-week ceasefire
video:video.video:description: President Donald Trump said late Tuesday he’s pulling back on his threats to launch devastating strikes on Iran (AP Production: Marissa Duhaney)

video:video.video:title: Pro-Iran government demonstrators burn US, Israeli flags in Tehran following news of ceasefire
video:video.video:description: Pro-government demonstrators gathered and chanted in the streets of Iran’s capital Wednesday morning after the ceasefire was announced. (AP video shot by Mohsen Ganji)
